metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `criteria/model.py:118` (internal self-call `_detector_matches(det.id, selector)`) to the edit targets so it is renamed to `detector_matches` in the same commit.
findings.finding: The rename of `_detector_matches` → `detector_matches` at criteria/model.py:90 also requires updating the internal self-call at criteria/model.py:118, which uses the old private name. This edit target is not named in the plan or ACs.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation section, append: 'Also update the internal self-call at `criteria/model.py:118` from `_detector_matches` to `detector_matches`.' Add a matching AC: '`criteria/model.py` contains no remaining references to `_detector_matches`.'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC: e.g. AC1/AC2: `grep -rn 'detector_matches' src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`; AC3: name the exact pytest invocation covering det_invariants (e.g. `pytest tests/llm/plan_review/test_det_invariants.py`).
findings.finding: No concrete proving commands are given for any of the three ACs. AC1 ('detector_matches is public in criteria.model') and AC2 ('noqa re-export in registry.py is removed') have no verification command at all; AC3 names 'tests green' without specifying which test command or test module covers the DET-invariant gate.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to each AC a one-line shell command:
- AC1: `python -c "from rebar.llm.criteria.model import detector_matches"` (exits 0 on success)
- AC2: `grep -n 'noqa' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py | grep detector` should return empty
- AC3: `pytest tests/llm/plan_review/test_det_invariants.py -q`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a step with a proving command) that asserts no remaining import of `_detector_matches` from `registry` exists: e.g. `grep -rn 'from.*registry.*import.*_detector_matches' src/` returns no results.
findings.finding: The cascade_flag acknowledges that there may be importers of `registry._detector_matches` beyond `det_invariants.py`, but no AC covers verifying that ALL such importers have been updated. Deleting the re-export shim without updating every importer will cause import errors not caught by the named ACs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`grep -rn "from.*registry.*import.*_detector_matches" src/` returns zero results after the sweep.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies no remaining import of `_detector_matches` from `registry` exists in the codebase (e.g., a grep/AST check or a confirmed-exhaustive importer list).
findings.finding: The cascade_flag describes additional work ('sweep importers of registry._detector_matches') but no AC covers verification that all other importers have been updated — creating uncovered described work.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'No import of `_detector_matches` from `rebar.llm.plan_review.registry` (or the private name from any module) remains in the codebase — confirmed by grep or equivalent check.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit expand-contract sequence (publish `detector_matches` first; confirm zero remaining uses of the old private path; then delete the shim) or a stated rollback step (e.g., 'revert commit X to restore the shim').
findings.finding: The plan removes the `registry.py` re-export (a path consumers rely on) without providing an expand-contract sequence or explicit rollback/back-out plan — acknowledgement via cascade_flag is insufficient under T4.
findings.suggested_fix: Structure the work as two steps: Step 1 — add public `detector_matches` alongside the private name (expand); Step 2 — after confirming all importers migrated (via grep AC), delete `_detector_matches` and the registry shim (contract). This makes Step 2 trivially revertible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a codebase-wide grep/audit for all `registry._detector_matches` (and `registry.detector_matches`) imports and confirming each is updated or removed before the shim is deleted.
findings.finding: The plan flags a cascade sweep ('sweep importers of registry._detector_matches') but none of the Acceptance Criteria require enumerating and updating every existing consumer of the old import path. If any module besides det_invariants.py imports _detector_matches from registry, it will break silently after the shim is removed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'grep -r "_detector_matches" src/ returns hits only in criteria.model and det_invariants.py (nowhere else); CI import-check passes on all packages.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies no file in the codebase retains an import of `_detector_matches` from `registry` (e.g., a grep/ast check in CI or an explicit AC line: 'no remaining reference to `registry._detector_matches` anywhere').
findings.finding: The cascade sweep ('sweep importers of registry._detector_matches') is a non-deferred goal stated in the plan, but the Acceptance Criteria only explicitly verify det_invariants.py as the updated consumer. If additional files import the old re-export path, they have no dedicated AC line confirming they were updated — reliance on 'tests green' is implicit and may not catch every consumer if test coverage is incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`git grep "registry._detector_matches"` (or equivalent AST check) returns no results after the change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `criteria/__init__.py` should re-export `detector_matches` in `__all__`; if yes, add it as an explicit edit target in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan titles the change 'Publish detector_matches as a public API' but does not name `rebar/llm/criteria/__init__.py` as an edit target. That module has an explicit `__all__` that currently omits `detector_matches`; without adding it there, the symbol is not part of the package's public surface.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/criteria/__init__.py / AC line 1 ('detector_matches is public in criteria.model')
findings.suggested_fix: If package-level export is intended, add to Remediation: 'Add `detector_matches` to `rebar/llm/criteria/__init__.py`'s `__all__` and its import list.' If module-level-only is sufficient, revise AC1 to 'public (no leading underscore) in `criteria.model`; not required at package level.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale to the plan explaining why `detector_matches` belongs on `criteria.model`'s public surface (e.g. it is consumed both internally by `_det_scenario` and externally by `det_invariants`, making it a genuine shared utility, not a linter workaround).
findings.finding: The plan states WHAT to do (rename, redirect, delete shim) but provides no positive rationale for WHY promotion to a public name is the right mechanism — e.g. that a cross-package import of a private symbol is an anti-pattern regardless of the noqa, and that the public name is the correct encapsulation boundary. Without this rationale an implementer cannot distinguish 'we are making this public because the function is genuinely part of the shared API' from 'we are making it public only to satisfy the linter'.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation section, add: 'Rationale: `detector_matches` is already called internally within `criteria.model` (by `_det_scenario` at line 118) and is a correct part of its public API — promotion expresses intent, not just lint compliance. Update `criteria/__init__.__all__` if the function should be reachable via the package facade.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the verbatim `## Reviewing stance` block from the plan body — it duplicates `_SHARED_PREAMBLE` (passes.py:246-253), which the pipeline already injects as system framing; its presence in plan content is a confirmed T8(e) pink-elephant antipattern.
findings.finding: The plan's opening '## Reviewing stance' block is verbatim identical to _SHARED_PREAMBLE (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:246-253), which _resolve_system() prepends to every plan-review system prompt. Because the pipeline also renders the full plan into the {{plan}} variable in that same system prompt, the preamble appears TWICE in the effective prompt — once as the injected stance framing and once embedded in the material under review. This is the instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern T8(e) explicitly flags: instruction-shaped prose inside the plan content can confuse an executor about whether it is a directive or evidence.
findings.location: Plan body, opening section '## Reviewing stance'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:245-273
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the '## Reviewing stance' section from the plan text entirely. The pipeline's _resolve_system() already prepends _SHARED_PREAMBLE to every reviewer's system prompt; the plan body should contain only the spec content (finding, remediation, ACs).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
